name: PALVELLA THERAPEUTICS, INC.
business: We are now a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and, if approved, commercializing novel therapies to treat patients suffering from serious, rare skin diseases and vascular malformations for which there are no FDA-approved therapies. Our lead product candidate, QTORIN 3.9% rapamycin anhydrous gel (QTORIN rapamycin), is currently in clinical development for microcystic lymphatic malformations and cutaneous venous malformations.
sellsTo: Patients with serious, rare skin diseases and vascular malformations
Healthcare providers including dermatologists and vascular anomaly specialists

exposures.sentence: Our lead product candidate, QTORIN 3.9% rapamycin anhydrous gel ( QTORIN rapamycin ), is currently in clinical development for microcystic lymphatic malformations ( microcystic LMs ) and cutaneous venous malformations ( cutaneous VMs ).

catalysts.event: Positive topline results from Phase 3 SELVA trial for microcystic LMs announced
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we announced positive topline results from SELVA, a Phase 3, single-arm, baseline-controlled study, which evaluated the safety and efficacy of QTORIN rapamycin for the treatment of microcystic LMs in patients 3 years and older.

catalysts.event: Positive topline efficacy results from Phase 2 TOIVA trial for cutaneous VMs announced
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we announced positive topline efficacy results from TOIVA, a Phase 2, single-arm, baseline-controlled study, which evaluated the safety and efficacy of QTORIN rapamycin for the treatment of cutaneous VMs in patients 6 years and older, which achieved nominal statistical significance (p 0.001) on multiple pre-specified clinician-reported and patient-reported efficacy endpoints, including dynamic change endpoints and static severity endpoints at Week 12.

catalysts.event: Expansion of QTORIN rapamycin development program into clinically significant angiokeratomas announced
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, we announced the expansion of our QTORIN rapamycin development program into clinically significant angiokeratomas.

catalysts.event: New product candidate QTORIN pitavastatin for DSAP announced
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, we announced a new QTORIN product candidate, QTORIN pitavastatin, for the treatment of disseminated superficial actinic porokeratosis.

catalysts.event: Pre-NDA meeting request submitted to FDA for QTORIN rapamycin in microcystic LMs
catalysts.sentence: In the first quarter of 2026, we submitted a pre-NDA meeting request to the FDA.
We anticipate the meeting to occur during the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Plan to submit NDA for QTORIN rapamycin for microcystic LMs
catalysts.sentence: We plan to submit an NDA to the FDA in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Plan to submit Breakthrough Therapy Designation application for QTORIN rapamycin in cutaneous VMs
catalysts.sentence: Our intent is to submit an application to the FDA for Breakthrough Therapy Designation in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Plan to commence Phase 3 pivotal study for QTORIN rapamycin in cutaneous VMs
catalysts.sentence: We plan to commence a Phase 3 pivotal study in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Phase 2 study initiation for QTORIN rapamycin in clinically significant angiokeratomas
catalysts.sentence: Study initiation is anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Phase 2 study initiation for QTORIN pitavastatin in DSAP
catalysts.sentence: Trial initiation is anticipated in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: It is estimated that there are more than 30,000 diagnosed patients in the United States with microcystic LMs.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that there are more than 30,000 diagnosed patients in the United States with microcystic LMs.

tamClaims.claim: It is estimated that there are more than 75,000 diagnosed patients in the United States with cutaneous VMs.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that there are more than 75,000 diagnosed patients in the United States with cutaneous VMs.

tamClaims.claim: No FDA-approved therapies currently exist for the estimated more than 50,000 diagnosed patients in the U.S. with clinically significant angiokeratomas.
tamClaims.sentence: No FDA-approved therapies currently exist for the estimated more than 50,000 diagnosed patients in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: No FDA-approved therapies currently exist for the estimated more than 50,000 diagnosed patients in the U.S. with DSAP.
tamClaims.sentence: No FDA-approved therapies currently exist for the estimated more than 50,000 diagnosed patients in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Based on 30,000 diagnosed patients in the U.S., we believe the estimated TAM opportunity on an annualized basis is greater than $1 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on 30,000 diagnosed patients in the U.S., we believe the estimated TAM opportunity on an annualized basis is greater than $1 billion
